fields.definition_origin: Carl Jung's 12 archetypes from 'The Archetypes and the Collective Unconscious' (1959), formalized for brand application by Margaret Mark and Carol S. Pearson in 'The Hero and the Outlaw: Building Extraordinary Brands Through the Power of Archetypes' (McGraw-Hill, 2001). Onboarding-specific archetypal voice application formalized in this research project's emotional-design research (.agent/research/emotional-design/results/Jungian_Brand_Archetypes.json) and synthesized with Susan Weinschenk's identity-based UX writing (2011), Nicole Fenton & Kate Kiefer Lee's 'Nicely Said' (2014), and Torrey Podmajersky's 'Strategic Writing for UX' (O'Reilly 2019). Archetype-conditioned onboarding patterns observed in 2024-2026 production apps (Headspace = Sage/Caregiver; Cal AI = Magician; Duolingo = Jester; Calm = Caregiver; Apple Fitness+ = Hero; Phantom Wallet = Magician; Linear = Sage/Creator).
fields.core_mechanism: Archetypes are pre-cognitive narrative patterns the right hemisphere matches within ~300ms, mapping a brand to a familiar story-role and reducing cognitive load. Onboarding is the user's first sustained encounter with the brand voice; voice consistency across 12+ screens compounds the archetypal slot, while mismatch (Sage app with Jester celebration, Caregiver app with Hero imperative copy) breaks the pattern-match and triggers low-effort / not-for-me trust decay (item AI_Slop_Tells.json forgettable-middle effect). The mechanism: voice is operationalized via four token-systems applied to every onboarding screen — (1) COPY REGISTER (vocabulary, sentence structure, person/tense, exclamation tolerance), (2) MOTION (easing curves, spring stiffness, celebration intensity), (3) TYPOGRAPHY (display family, body family, weight range), (4) COLOR (surface, ink, accent palette). Each archetype gets a 1-line onboarding voice spec, ban-list of phrases, and prescribed-list. Mismatch is not aesthetic preference — it's measurable on retention/NPS for archetypally consistent brands (Y&R BAV: 97% higher esteem, Mark & Pearson 2001).
fields.neuroscience_correlate: Archetypal recognition theorized to engage default mode network (autobiographical narrative processing) and medial prefrontal cortex (self-referential meaning) — Yoon et al. 2006 fMRI study of brand vs person judgments. Direct fMRI evidence for the 12-archetype taxonomy is limited (uncertain).
fields.empirical_evidence: (1) Mark & Pearson (2001): Y&R BrandAsset Valuator, n=13,000+ brands across 33 countries — archetypally consistent brands had ~97% higher 'esteem' and 66% higher 'differentiation' than muddled. Caveat: proprietary corporate research. (2) Tsai (2006), Web J. Chinese Mgmt Review, n=486: archetype clarity correlated r=0.42 with brand-loyalty intent. (3) Lee et al. (2019), J. Brand Mgmt, n=312: archetype-self-congruence predicted purchase intent β=0.31. (4) Pearson PMAI psychometric studies: Cronbach's α 0.65-0.85 across the 12 scales. (5) Industry case studies: Dove Caregiver pivot (2004-2014) = 700% sales lift to $6B; Harley-Davidson Outlaw consistency = 80%+ owner re-purchase loyalty. (6) Onboarding-specific 2024-2026: Headspace (Sage/Caregiver) D30 retention >50%; Duolingo (Jester) viral notification engagement >20% open rate; Cal AI (Magician) $30M Y1 ARR. Industry-benchmark, not RCT for the onboarding-specific application.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: Brand-recall in 5-second test (uxtweak / fivesecondtest), NPS at onboarding completion, sentiment analysis on App Store reviews (archetype-coherent brands trend more positive), onboarding completion rate (archetype-consistent voice correlates with higher completion), retention curves D7/D30 by archetype-consistency cohort.
fields.evidence_strength: industry_benchmark + practitioner_opinion (Mark & Pearson proprietary; academic validation thinner)
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: Y&R BAV: 97% higher esteem score for archetype-consistent brands (n=13K). Specific onboarding-conversion lift from archetype-tuned voice not publicly RCT-validated; inferred from brand-recall and retention proxies.
fields.engineering_tactics:
BUILD A PER-ARCHETYPE VOICE+VISUAL TABLE. Below: 12 archetypes, each with 1-line onboarding voice + 2-3 banned phrases + 1-2 prescribed phrases.

(1) MAGICIAN — desire: transformation/wonder. ONBOARDING VOICE: Mystical, allusive, reveals-rather-than-explains; speaks of possibility unfolding. BAN: 'just', 'simply', 'easy', '🎉', generic 'Welcome!', '✨ AI-powered', exclamation marks on declarative copy. PRESCRIBE: 'See what's possible', 'Watch this transform', 'Step into the threshold'. MOTION: morphing curves, particle dissolves, iridescent gradients (deep midnight #0B0E2A → #7C3AED → #EC4899), 0.4s ease-in-out, NEVER overshoot. TYPOGRAPHY: Cabinet Grotesk display 56pt + IBM Plex Sans body. CELEBRATION: subtle iridescent particle dissolve, never confetti. EXAMPLE APPS: Cal AI, Phantom Wallet, Apple (product launches), Tesla, Mau Baron Prayer Lock.

(2) HERO — desire: mastery/courage. ONBOARDING VOICE: Declarative, confident, action-oriented, second-person imperatives. BAN: hedge words ('maybe', 'perhaps', 'we'll try'), passive voice, multi-clause sentences, soft pastels. PRESCRIBE: 'Push through', 'Earn your day', 'Show up'. MOTION: punchy ease-in-out 0.2s, ascending arcs, bold contrast. TYPOGRAPHY: Druk / Founders Grotesk Bold / Inter Black 60pt+ headlines. COLOR: high-contrast primary (red #E11D48 or electric blue #1D4ED8 on black). CELEBRATION: bold ascending burst, one strong haptic. EXAMPLE APPS: Apple Fitness+, Nike Training Club, Strava, Whoop.

(3) CAREGIVER — desire: service/protection. ONBOARDING VOICE: Nurturing, reassuring, gentle pacing, no pressure. BAN: imperatives ('Do this'), urgency markers ('Now!', 'Today only'), exclamation marks, loss-framed ('Don't lose'), 'Crushing it'. PRESCRIBE: 'We'll be here when you're ready', 'Take your time', 'You're not alone'. MOTION: cradling ease-out 0.4s, soft springs (damping 18, stiffness 100), no abrupt stops. TYPOGRAPHY: Nunito / Mulish / Source Sans rounded humanist. COLOR: soft teal / blush / sage (#5EAAA8, #F5C2C7, #B5C9A4). CELEBRATION: warm-color soft scale-in with reassuring copy. EXAMPLE APPS: Calm, Headspace (Caregiver-Sage hybrid), BetterHelp, Talkspace.

(4) SAGE — desire: truth/understanding. ONBOARDING VOICE: Measured, evidence-led, calm; cites studies, uses third-person, allows the data to speak. BAN: emoji, hype words ('amazing', 'incredible', 'magical'), exclamation marks, gradient typography, 'Let's go!'. PRESCRIBE: 'Research shows', 'In a 2024 study', 'Three findings'. MOTION: minimal, instant settle, no bounce, 0.15s linear or ease-out. TYPOGRAPHY: Söhne / GT Sectra / Tiempos refined serif or transitional sans. COLOR: ink/parchment (#1B1C20, #F4EDE0). CELEBRATION: minimal acknowledgment, small text-only confirmation. EXAMPLE APPS: Linear (Sage-Creator), Notion (Sage-Everyman), MasterClass, Curiosity.

(5) JESTER — desire: joy/play. ONBOARDING VOICE: Surprising, irreverent, witty; callbacks to user input, second-person teasing. BAN: corporate-speak ('leverage', 'optimize', 'solution'), serious tone, somber colors, monochrome celebrations. PRESCRIBE: puns, callbacks ('Remember when you said...'), 'Hey, you came back!', emoji-as-punctuation (NOT bullets). MOTION: anticipatory squash-and-stretch, 0.6s with overshoot bounce (damping 8, stiffness 180). TYPOGRAPHY: Recoleta / Fraktion / Mona variable axes. COLOR: candy-bright primaries (#FFD600, #FF4081, #00BFA5). CELEBRATION: full confetti, mascot animation, sound effects. EXAMPLE APPS: Duolingo, Old Spice, Headspace (Jester-accent on serious base), Mailchimp.

(6) OUTLAW — desire: revolution/disruption. ONBOARDING VOICE: Defiant, raw, sardonic; anti-establishment social proof; pre-empts cynicism. BAN: corporate niceness, '🚀', 'Welcome aboard', cheerful pastels, generic confetti, 'Trusted by Fortune 500'. PRESCRIBE: 'Burn it down', 'The system is broken', 'You weren't built for that anyway'. MOTION: jagged off-grid, intentional broken-easing cubic-bezier(0.85,0,0.15,1). TYPOGRAPHY: Druk Wide / Akzidenz / custom glitch / stencil. COLOR: black + violent accent (#000 + #FF1744 or acid green #00E676). CELEBRATION: glitch effect, distorted typography burst, no smiles. EXAMPLE APPS: Liquid Death, Patagonia (Outlaw-Explorer), Diesel, early Robinhood pre-2021 pivot.

(7) LOVER — desire: intimacy/sensuality. ONBOARDING VOICE: Warm, intimate, sensory; uses 'feel', 'savor', 'linger'; speaks one-on-one. BAN: utility-speak ('efficient', 'productive'), monochrome cold, 'Get started in seconds', urgency. PRESCRIBE: 'Linger here', 'Feel into this', 'We've been waiting'. MOTION: slow languid 0.5s ease-in-out, lingering hover states, no sudden cuts. TYPOGRAPHY: Playfair / Canela / GT Super high-contrast didone. COLOR: blush / burgundy / soft gold (#F8C8DC, #800020, #D4AF37). CELEBRATION: slow bloom animation, warm gradient fade. EXAMPLE APPS: Hinge, Bumble (Lover-Caregiver), Pure, dating apps generally; high-end fashion (Chanel, Net-a-Porter).

(8) INNOCENT — desire: safety/happiness. ONBOARDING VOICE: Warm, simple, optimistic, Crayola-grade clarity, no jargon. BAN: cynicism, ambiguity, dark colors, 'However', 'unfortunately', complex multi-clause sentences. PRESCRIBE: 'Let's start!', 'Hi there!', 'You're going to love this'. MOTION: gentle ease-out spring 0.3s, no aggressive overshoot. TYPOGRAPHY: Quicksand / Nunito / DM Sans rounded humanist. COLOR: pastel cream / sky / butter (#FFF8E7, #B8E0D2). CELEBRATION: soft sparkles, friendly mascot. EXAMPLE APPS: Disney+, Nintendo Mini, ABCmouse, Khan Academy Kids.

(9) EXPLORER — desire: freedom/discovery. ONBOARDING VOICE: Rugged, authentic, restless; speaks of paths, horizons, finding-your-way. BAN: 'comfortable', 'familiar', 'safe', overly-finished aesthetics, sleek minimalism. PRESCRIBE: 'Find your way', 'Where would you go?', 'Off the map'. MOTION: parallax, distance metaphors, horizontal scroll. TYPOGRAPHY: condensed grotesk (Bebas Neue, Tungsten) or geometric (Druk). COLOR: earth + horizon (terra #B85C38, sky #4A7C95). CELEBRATION: subtle map-marker drop, compass-rotation. EXAMPLE APPS: AllTrails, Strava (Explorer-Hero), Komoot, Airbnb (Explorer-Everyman), REI.

(10) CREATOR — desire: self-expression/originality. ONBOARDING VOICE: Imaginative, articulate, particular; uses 'craft', 'compose', 'design'; values precision. BAN: generic templates, 'modern minimal', 'effortless', stock visuals. PRESCRIBE: 'Compose your own', 'Bring it to the surface', 'No template required'. MOTION: deliberate, slightly slower than spec (0.35s), purposeful. TYPOGRAPHY: editorial serif (GT Sectra) + monospace counterpoint (JetBrains Mono). COLOR: gallery-white + single bold pigment (#FFFFFF + #FF5722). CELEBRATION: subtle reveal of created artifact, Polaroid-drop. EXAMPLE APPS: Procreate, Figma, Notion (Creator-Everyman), Adobe, Are.na.

(11) RULER — desire: control/order/prestige. ONBOARDING VOICE: Authoritative, refined, certain; institutional third-person; uses 'standard', 'unparalleled'. BAN: casual ('hey', 'cool'), emoji, gradients, novelty fonts. PRESCRIBE: 'The standard for serious work', 'Used by leading firms', 'Built for those who lead'. MOTION: stately 0.4s ease-out, no playfulness, no spring overshoot. TYPOGRAPHY: classic serif or geometric capitals (Trajan, Optima, Domaine). COLOR: deep ink, burgundy, gold leaf (#1A1A2E, #722F37, #C9A961). CELEBRATION: minimal gold-line drawer. EXAMPLE APPS: Wealthfront, premium fintech, Mercedes me, Bloomberg Terminal.

(12) EVERYMAN — desire: belonging. ONBOARDING VOICE: Plainspoken, modest, unpretentious; uses 'we', 'us', conversational. BAN: superlatives ('best', 'greatest'), exclusivity language, emoji-as-decoration. PRESCRIBE: 'Most of us', 'We get it', 'You're not the only one'. MOTION: standard 0.2s ease, no flourish. TYPOGRAPHY: utilitarian humanist (Inter, Helvetica, SF Pro) — yes Inter is FINE for Everyman archetype. COLOR: denim + warm neutrals (#3B5998, #F5F5F4). CELEBRATION: simple checkmark, friendly toast. EXAMPLE APPS: Notion (Everyman-Creator), Reddit, Trello, Mailchimp (Everyman-Jester), Target.

IMPLEMENTATION TACTICS: (1) PICK ONE PRIMARY archetype + at most ONE SECONDARY (Hero+Caregiver = 'tough love' trainer; Sage+Creator = 'thoughtful craftsman'). Reject 'a bit of everything'. (2) Codify the archetype into a 1-page voice/visual brief with banned + prescribed lists. (3) Encode in design tokens (theme.archetype = 'magician'; theme.archetype.fontHeading = 'Cabinet Grotesk'). (4) SDUI (item 31) theme_tokens carry archetype config, enabling per-archetype variants in same binary. (5) Lint rules: forbid emoji in onboarding/ folder for non-Jester/Innocent archetypes; forbid Inter for non-Everyman; forbid indigo-500 universally. (6) Voice review on every copy change — archetype-owner must approve. (7) A/B test (item 45) archetype-conditioned variants for category fit on cohort LTV. (8) Archetype-conditioned celebration component library (item 51 Generic Celebration fix).
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Cal AI (Magician): theatrical AI plan reveal, mystical loader copy ('Cross-referencing 2.4M users'), iridescent gradient hero. (2) Headspace (Sage+Caregiver): Andy Puddicombe voice, calm orange tones, evidence-led copy ('Studies show'), gentle Andy-mascot animations. (3) Duolingo (Jester): Duo mascot personality, 'Don't make Duo cry' (problematic per item 51), confetti, sound effects, callback humor in notifications. (4) Calm (Caregiver): blue-night palette, 'You can sleep tonight', no urgency, gentle unfurling content. (5) Strava (Hero+Explorer): 'Earn your weekend', achievement badges, athletic typography. (6) Nike Training Club (Hero): swoosh, 'Just Do It' lineage, athlete imagery, declarative imperatives. (7) Procreate (Creator): minimal chrome, gallery-white + bold accent, Apple Pencil emphasis. (8) Liquid Death (Outlaw): 'Murder your thirst', skull iconography, anti-bottled-water punk register. (9) Hinge (Lover): 'Designed to be deleted', warm tones, intimate one-on-one copy. (10) Apple Fitness+ (Hero+Innocent hybrid): trainer-led optimism, achievement focus.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile constrains archetype expression to micro-moments — app icon (single archetype symbol), splash 1.2s archetype-set, haptic personality. Web has more surface area for narrative — hero video, scroll-driven storytelling, hover micro-interactions. Onboarding-specific: mobile's haptic channel can carry archetype (Outlaw uses Impact.heavy, Caregiver uses Selection.light, Jester uses Notification.success); web has no haptic equivalent. Sound on mobile is mute-respecting; web sound less common. Voice consistency more critical on mobile due to higher session frequency.
fields.platform_design_language_alignment: Liquid Glass (iOS 26+) leans Magician/Lover (translucent, sensual, mystical) — fights Everyman/Outlaw rawness; override at brand layer. Material 3 Expressive (Android 16+) leans Jester/Creator (vibrant Material You, springy motion, expressive typography axes) — fights Sage/Ruler restraint. Ship-faster guidance: if archetype aligns with platform default, embrace; if conflicts, override deliberately at theme layer (custom fonts, motion curves, color tokens) and accept higher engineering cost.
fields.category_fit: Each archetype maps to category-default fits but is not deterministic. WELLNESS/HABIT: Caregiver / Magician (transformation arc) / Sage. AI-WRAPPER: Magician dominant. FITNESS: Hero / Explorer / Caregiver (gentle wellness). EDTECH: Sage / Jester (Duolingo) / Creator. FINTECH: Sage / Ruler / Everyman (rarely Magician — feels predatory). DATING: Lover / Caregiver / Innocent / Jester. JOURNALING: Sage / Creator / Lover. PRODUCTIVITY B2B: Sage / Creator / Everyman / Ruler (rarely Jester or Outlaw). GAMING: Hero / Jester / Outlaw / Magician. UTILITY: Everyman / Sage. NOVEL CATEGORIES: Magician / Explorer / Outlaw (be category-creator).
fields.solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week. Author voice/visual brief (1-2 days), encode tokens (1 day), audit existing copy + visuals against brief (1-2 days), iterate (ongoing). Solo dev with single archetype can ship coherent onboarding faster than archetype-confused team.
fields.tooling_options: VOICE/CONTENT: Markdown brief + Notion / Figma / shared doc. DESIGN TOKENS: Style Dictionary, Tokens Studio for Figma, theme objects in code. ARCHETYPE ASSESSMENT: Pearson PMAI (paid, ~$45 individual), Kaye Putnam's free archetype quiz. COPY EDITING: Hemingway Editor (readability), Grammarly (tone), custom GPT trained on the voice brief. AUDIT: human review + Claude/GPT-4 cross-check ('does this copy match Magician voice?'). NO 'archetype management platform' exists — this is craft.
fields.ai_dependency: none. Optional: AI copy-generation conditioned on archetype voice spec (provide spec verbatim in prompt).
fields.claude_design_application: Instruct Claude with archetype as a primary token. Prompt fragment 1: 'Build the React Native onboarding flow for Kleeve. Brand archetype: MAGICIAN. Voice: mystical, allusive, transforms-rather-than-explains. Tokens: font-heading Cabinet Grotesk, font-body Söhne, color.surface #F4EFE6, color.ink #1A1816, color.accent #E36B2C (warm ember as the spell-color). Motion: 0.4s ease-in-out, NEVER overshoot. FORBID: "just", "simply", "easy", emoji, exclamation marks on declarative copy, generic Welcome! header, ✨ as bullet, 🎉 as celebration, indigo-500 / purple-blue gradient, Inter typeface. PRESCRIBE: "See what's possible", "Watch this transform", "Step into the threshold", subtle iridescent dissolve celebration, particle systems for AI plan reveal. Output the design tokens FIRST as JSON, then the components.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Audit every onboarding copy line against the Magician archetype ban list. Output a per-line table: [line | matches archetype? | violation? | fix]. Forbid copy that reads more Caregiver/Sage/Jester than Magician.' Prompt fragment 3: 'Generate the AI plan reveal screen. Magician voice — the reveal is the spell. Use 4-7s theatrical loader (item 13) with morphing copy: "Listening for the pattern" → "Aligning the threads" → "Here's what wants to come through". The reveal animation: iridescent particle dissolve from center, 1.2s, no confetti. Headline 64pt Cabinet Grotesk weight 350, color #1A1816 on cream surface #F4EFE6, single ember accent on the plan name.'
fields.stitch_application: Stitch must be told exact archetype tokens. Prompt fragment 1: 'Mockup onboarding for a CAREGIVER-archetype mental-health app. Surface: warm sage #B5C9A4 with cream overlay #F8F4ED. Typography: Nunito Bold 28px headline (rounded humanist), Mulish Regular 15px body. Card border-radius 24px (soft), shadow 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.06). CTA copy reads "We'll be here when you're ready" — never use exclamation marks or imperatives. Avatar illustration is muted, two-tone, gentle facial expression. Forbid: red/orange urgency, "Now!", "Today only", "Don't lose", emoji bullets, sharp angular illustrations.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Mockup the same onboarding for a MAGICIAN archetype variant. Surface: deep midnight #0B0E2A with cream highlight type #F4EFE6. Typography: Cabinet Grotesk 56pt headline weight 350, IBM Plex Sans 14px body. Card border-radius 18px, no shadow but iridescent edge gradient. CTA copy reads "Step into the threshold". Avatar replaced with abstract orb illustration that morphs slightly. Same content, completely different feel — proves archetype-conditioned theming.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI defaults to a generic 'modern SaaS' voice — Inter typeface, indigo accent, 'Get started in seconds!' copy, ✨ emoji bullets, 0.2s ease-in-out, stock confetti — REGARDLESS of what archetype you specify. AI conflates archetypes (renders Outlaw as 'dark mode' instead of intentional rule-breaking; Jester as 'colorful' instead of timing-based humor; Sage as 'serif font' instead of evidentiary copy register). Counter-prompt: 'Treat the archetype as a constraint, not a flavor. FORBID Inter unless archetype is Everyman. FORBID blue primary unless archetype is Sage/Ruler. FORBID ✨ 🎉 🚀 emoji unless archetype is Jester or Innocent. FORBID exclamation marks unless archetype is Innocent or Jester. FORBID confetti animation unless archetype is Jester or (Innocent and milestone). Specify motion easing per archetype: Caregiver=ease-out 0.4s, Jester=spring overshoot, Sage=instant, Magician=morphing 0.4s no bounce. Output the design tokens as JSON FIRST, then component code, so I can verify archetype consistency before reading JSX.' AI also gives lip-service archetype tokens then writes generic copy in the JSX — counter: 'every copy line must pass the archetype ban list; output a per-line audit before generating components.'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) Generic Celebration (item 51 #8) — stock Lottie confetti regardless of archetype. (2) Aspirational mismatch — founder picks 'Magician' for what's actually a Sage/Everyman utility. (3) Trying to be 'a bit of everything' — forgettable middle. (4) Veneer-only archetype — copy reads Outlaw, UI reads corporate. (5) Cultural mistranslation — Outlaw plays as 'edgy' in US, can read as disrespectful in JP/KR; Caregiver as 'warm' in West, paternalistic in Nordic. (6) Static archetype expression — never refreshing as platform conventions evolve. (7) Archetype hijacking — layering Jester emoji-spam on a Sage finance product because 'engagement'. (8) Inter typeface forced on every archetype because 'safe default' (Inter is appropriate ONLY for Everyman). (9) Indigo-500 as universal accent regardless of archetype.
fields.regulatory_risk: Low for archetype itself. Amplifies risk in adjacent areas: (a) Outlaw + fintech/crypto + investment-advice copy → SEC/FCA scrutiny. (b) Caregiver + health + medical-advice phrasing → FDA Software-as-Medical-Device exposure. (c) Jester + under-18 audiences + nudge mechanics → UK AADC + COPPA dark-pattern flags. (d) Lover + dating/social → DSA Article 28 minor protection; consent. (e) Magician + AI claims ('predict your future') → FTC § 5 deception risk if not factually defensible.
fields.platform_constraints_2026: No platform forbids any archetype. Apple Liquid Glass and Material 3 Expressive bias toward certain archetypes (Magician/Lover vs Jester/Creator respectively); override with custom tokens. Apple Guideline 4.3 'Spam' could be invoked if your Outlaw clone-app is one of N me-too entries — archetype consistency itself helps differentiate. EU DFA Q4 2026 focuses on dark patterns regardless of archetype; archetype voice doesn't excuse manipulation.
fields.accessibility_implications: Archetype expression must not violate a11y. Highly stylized fonts (Outlaw distorted, Magician script) often fail screen-reader-friendly defaults — pair archetypal display fonts with accessible body fonts (Söhne, IBM Plex). High-contrast Hero / Outlaw palettes generally meet WCAG 1.4.3 (4.5:1) easily; Lover blush palettes may fail — verify. Caregiver's soft sage-on-cream may fall below contrast ratio — boost ink color. Jester's celebration-heavy motion respects prefers-reduced-motion (replace spring with instant). VoiceOver/TalkBack: archetype mascots need accessibilityLabel ('Andy mascot, smiling' for Headspace's Andy). Dynamic Type: display fonts must scale.
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) Archetype theory is largely proprietary marketing knowledge with limited peer-reviewed validation; treat 'effect sizes' from agency case studies skeptically. (2) Cultural variation significant — see cross-cultural-validity. (3) Founder-archetype-mismatch — solo dev's personality may not align with intended brand archetype; brief must be voice-document, not personality-document. (4) Performance: archetype-rich motion (Magician particles, Jester springs) costs frame budget on low-end Android. (5) Maintenance: voice drift over time as multiple writers contribute; central voice review mandatory. (6) Copy translations may break archetype voice — localize per archetype, not just literal translation. (7) Typography licensing for archetype-specific fonts (Cabinet Grotesk, GT Sectra) is non-trivial cost (~$200-2000 per font family). (8) Hybrid archetypes (Hero+Caregiver = 'tough love') work but require explicit blend rules.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: Archetypes claimed universal (Jung's collective unconscious thesis); expression varies significantly. Hero in US: triumphant individualist; in JP: collectivist (Hero serves group, Toyota Kaizen). Outlaw in US: romanticized rebel; in CN/SG: antisocial; Xiaomi softens to Everyman+Creator hybrid. Caregiver in MENA: aligns with Islamic Karam hospitality (very strong fit). Lover in conservative-modesty markets (KSA, parts of SE Asia): refinement/elegance, NOT sensuality. RTL (Arabic, Hebrew): Hero's forward-arrow ascending arc must mirror; Magician spell-language adapts to local mystical traditions. Recommendation: validate archetype expression with native designers per locale before launch. JP Caregiver register: more formal honorifics; Jester: subtler humor (less in-your-face).
fields.prescribed_action: Pick ONE primary archetype + at most ONE secondary. Author a 1-page voice/visual brief with banned + prescribed phrases, fonts, colors, motion curves, celebration patterns. Encode as design tokens (theme.archetype + per-archetype theme bundle). Audit every onboarding copy line and visual against the brief before ship. CI lint: forbid emoji for non-Jester/Innocent archetypes, forbid Inter for non-Everyman, forbid indigo-500 universally. A/B test (item 45) archetype-conditioned variants on cohort LTV when traffic supports. Forbid: 'a bit of everything' archetype confusion, veneer-only archetype (copy ≠ visuals), aspirational mismatch.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: Direct — questionnaire copy (items 26-28) tunes per archetype: Caregiver gentle reassurance per painpoint question, Magician transformation language per goal question, Sage evidence-led framing per data question, Hero declarative imperatives. Question types (item 27) preserved across archetypes; voice register varies.
fields.aha_relevance: Direct — AI plan reveal (item 12) is the canonical archetype expression peak. Magician: theatrical reveal as the spell. Caregiver: gentle reveal with reassurance. Sage: data-grounded plan reveal. Hero: triumphant reveal. Generic confetti reveal regardless of archetype = item 51 antipattern #8.
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: This item IS the archetype framework. Self-aligning per archetype.
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against item 51 antipattern #8 (Generic Celebration). Pairs with item AI_Slop_Tells.json (forbidden defaults regardless of archetype). Pairs with item 27 (Question Type Taxonomy) — archetype tunes the copy register of each question type. Pairs with item 12-13 (AI Plan Reveal + Theatrical Loader) — archetype shapes the loader copy and reveal animation.
sources: [The Hero and the Outlaw — Mark & Pearson (Amazon)](https://www.amazon.com/Hero-Outlaw-Building-Extraordinary-Archetypes/dp/0071364153)
uncertain: neuroscience_correlate (limited direct fMRI evidence for 12-archetype taxonomy)
specific RCT-grade onboarding-conversion lift figures from archetype-tuned voice
